Farn Polrrrcal PRACTTcEs CovrrrarssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95g14_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0986
November 13,2006
Dear Mr. Stevens and Mr. yonehiro:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the filing of
statements of economic interests ("SEIs') under the conflict-or-intelst ,oa"lrorisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').I 'Yourrequest 
involves the californii Bay-Delta
Authority (CBDA) and the Resources Agency 1a,ra constifuent departments Department
of Fish and Game, Department ofForestry andFire protection, ani t6;;ent 
of
later Resources) arising from the recent reorganizatron of the CBDA and the calFED
Bay-Delta Program (CALFED program).
QUESTTONS
Chris Stevens
Chief Counsel
CALFED B ay -D elta pro gram
650 Capitol Mall, 5fr Floor
Sacramento, CA g59l4
Bruce Yonehiro
Acting Deputy Secretary and General Counsel
The Resources Agency
650 Capitol Mall, 5th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-06-176
1' what are the duties of agencies to which CBDA staff was transferred,
including duties, if any, to amend their respective conflict-of-interest codes to cover new
positions?
I Government code sections 81000-91014. commission regulations appear attitle 2,sectiors
18109-18997, of the california-c^ode of Regulations. All further refeLces to statutory,.sections,, will be
to the Government code and all further references to "regulations" will be to title 2of the califomia code
of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-06-176
Page 2
2. Whatare the duties ,irfr; CnOa,designated employees,,who were
transferred to other agencies, including duties, if any, to file leaving office statements
with CBDA and assuming office statements with their new agencies, even if their scope
of work has not significantly changed?
3. What are the duties of CBDA "consultants," the underlying contracts for
whom were transferred to other agencies, including duties, if any, to file leaving office
statements with CBDA and assuming office statements with their new contracting
agencies, even if their scope of work has not significantly changed?
4. Are there any changes to the Form 700 filing duties of CBDA board members?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Agencies to which CBDA public officials transferred must amend their
conflict of interest codes to include the newly-transferred positions.
2 and 3. Non-board member public officials and consultants transferring from
CBDA to other agencies but with no material change in job duties, should continue to file
annual statements under the old conflict-of-interest code for CBDA until the agencies
satistr the code amendment requirements of the Act. We caution you that we do not
advise in this letter whether arly specific official has experienced a change in his or her
duties altering the official,s filing requirernents under the Act.
4. CBDA board members should continue to file annual statements under
CBDA's conflict-of-interest code. CBDA must amend its conflict-of-interest code to
remove positions that have transferred to other agencies.
FACTS
CDBA was established, effective January 1,2003, as a separate state entity within
the Resources Agency and charged with the oversight and coordination of the CALFED
Bay-Delta Program (see, generally, California Bay-Delta Authority Act, Division 26.4
(commencing with Section 79400, Water Code)). CBDA is governed by a24-member
board comprised of specified state and federal agency heads, appointed public members,
the chair of a CALFED advisoryboard, and ex-officio legislative membirs. The CBDA
board members, as applicable, file statements of economic interests (Form 700) pursuant
to the CBDA's conflict-of-interest code.
CBDA, until the reorganization described below, had its own employees to staff
the CBDA board and to perform other CALFED-related duties, some of whtm had Form
700 filing duties as "designated employees" pursuant to CBDA,s conflict-of-interest
code' CBDA also had under contract several persons who had Form 700 filing duties
because they were determined to be o'consultants" under CBDA,s conflict-of-interest
code. Effective July 1,2006, CBDA and the CALFED Program were reorgarrtzedby the


File No. A-06-176
Page 3
Legislature pursuant to the 2006-07 Budget Act and an accompanyrng budget trailer bill,
AB 1803 (ch.77, Stats. 2006). The Budget Act, among other things,-traosierr"d
CBDA's staff byprogram area focus to specified agen"ies thatpaiicipate in the
CALFED Program - the Resources Agency (32positions, including iBDA,, executive
management team), Department of Forestry andFire Protection (19 positions),
Department of Fish and Game (8 positions), Department of water Reso.rrc"s (3
positions), and the State Water Resources Control Board (2 positions). The trailer bill
made corresponding statutory changes regardingthe duties of th"r" alencies relative to
the CBDA board and the CALFED Program foliowing the transfer oith"r" positions.
In particular, the trailer bill (Sec. 61) added a new Section 79422 to the Water
Code, which reads as follows:
"79442. (a) It is the intent of the Legisrature, in enacting this
section, that all public processes that are currently part of the
Bay-Delta Program as of the date of enactment of the act adding
this section will continue, consistent with the actthatadded this
section, unless legislation is enacted to change these processes.
"(b) The Secretary of the Resources Agency shall provide
staff support to the authority to assist the authority in exercising
its duties under this division.
"(c) The Department of Forestry and Fire protection shafl
provide administrative support to assist the secretary of the
Resources Agency in carrying out the duties assigned to the
secretary by this section. That administrative support includes,
but is not limited to, all of the following:
"(1) Contracting and purchasing.
"(2) Budgeting and accounting.
"(3) Information technology.
"(4) Business services.
"(5) Human resources.
"(d) (1) Except as provided inparagraph e),the Secretary
of the Resources Agency shall administer ail contracts, grants,
leases and agreements under this division.
"(2) The Department of Fish and Game shafl administer alr
contracts, grants, leases, or agreements that relate to the
implementation of the ecosystem restoration program under this
division.
"(3) The exercise of the authority described in paragraphs
(1) and (2) shall not be subject to review or approval ty ttre
Department of General Services.
"(4) No contract, lease, license, or any other agreement to
which the authority is a party shall become void or vofuable as a
result of the implementation of this subdivision, but shall continue
in full force and effect.


File No. A-06-176
Page 4
"(e) The Secretary of the Resources Agency shall assume
from the authority all of the administrative rights, abilities,
obligations, and duties of the authority, except those rights,
abilities obligations, and duties assigned to the Department of Fish
and Game by this section.
"(f) The Secretary of the Resources Agency shall have
possession and control of all records, papers, equipment, supplies
and contracts, leases, agreements, and other property, real or
personal, connected with the administration of this division, or
held for the benefit or use of the authority.
"(g) All positions transferred from the authority in the
Budget Act of 2006 shall be used to support the Bay-Delta
Program or the secretary of the Resources Agency. The status,
position, and rights of any officer or employee shall not be affected
by this transfer and all officers and employees shall be retained
pursuant to the applicable provisions of the State civil Service Act
(Parl2 (commencing with Section 18500) of Division 5 of Title 2
of the Government code), except as to anyposition that is exempt
from civil service."
Following the reorganization, then, CBDA continues to exist as a separate entity
within the Resources Agency, and its board remains operational. However, its staff
positions, contracts, and associated funding have been reallocated to other daffgO
agencies effective July 2,2006. The reallocated staff continues to support the CBDA
board and the CALFED Program, primarily through the core management team
transferred to Resources Agency. For the most part, individual duties of reallocated staff
have not changed in any significant manner (and, in fact, with one exception to date, their
physical work location has not changed either.)
The reallocation of contracts, including consultancy contracts, from CBDA to
either the Resources Agency or the Department of Fish and Game, consistent with
subdivisions (d) and (e) of new Water Code Section 79422 (set forth above), has been or
will be effectuated bymeans of standard contract assignments executed byiie
contractors and the relevant agencies. In essence, the Resources Agency or the
Department of Fish and Game, as the case maybe, will be assuming all rights and
obligations of CBDA in the relevant contracts. Those contracts-all of which relate to
the CALFED Program-will remain in full force and effect and the relevant scopes of
work will not be changed by the assignments.
In short, the practical effect of the reorgarization-which is widely viewed as an
"interim" solution to more comprehensive reform of GALFED gor"111urrl in the
future-is that the same persons who worked on the CALFED Progrr- for CBDA will
continue to do so; only for administrative purposes they will report to new agencies. The
same holds true for CALFED contracts, which will merely be assigned fromtBDA to
new agencies without any substantive changes.


File No. A-06-176
Page 5
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that the assets and income of public officials which may be
matenally affected by their official actions be fully disclosed and, in appropriate
circumstances, the officials should be disqualified from actingin ordei ihaiconflicts of
interests may be avoided. (Section 8I002(c).) '?ublic officiils, whether elected or
appointed, should perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by
their own financial interests or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported
them." (Section 81001(b).) To accomplish these prrrpos"., the Act requires p"uri"
officials who make or participate in the making of govemmental decisions to file
statements of economic interests whereby they disclose their economic interests.
(Section 87300 et seq.; regulations 18701 and rg702.2.) Individuals holding
governmental positions under this filing requirement fall into two separate categories.
First, persons holding positions covered under section 87200 *" "orr.id".ed 
.,statutory
filers" and they must disclose all of their economic interests. The second category of
filers, governed by section 87300 et seq., requires positions within the agencyit i"t
involve the making or participation in the making of decisions which miy foieseeably
have a mateial effect on any financial interest to be included in a conflici-of-interest
code.
Governmental agencies are required to adopt and promulgate conflict-of-interest
codes which must contain a list of designated empl,oyees and the-corresponding
disclosure categories that assign disclosure based on the duties associated with their
positions. (Sections 82019 and, 87302.)
The basic question you pose is whether CBDA's staff and consultants must file a
leaving office statemelt for their prior CBDA positions and an assuming office with the
new agencies to which they transferred. Of particular note is that Water-Code Section
79a22@;) states that all positions transferred from the authority in the Budget Act of 2006
shall be used to support the Bay-Delta Program or the Secretary of the Res-ources
Agency. The status, position, and rights of any officer or empltyee shall not be affected
by this transfer and all officers and employees shall be retained pursuant to the applicable
provisions of the State Civil Service Act (Part 2 (commencing with Section 1g500) of
Division 5 of Title 2 of the Govemment Code), except as to anyposition that is exempt
from civil service. Additionally, Water Code Sectioils+zz6)14; rtut"r that no contract,
lease, license, or any other agreement to which the authority is'a party, shall become void
or voidable as a result of the implementation of this subdivision, but shall continue in full
force and effect.
The Commission has previously advised under similar circumstances that officials
with similar jobs continue to file annual SEIs under their old conflict-of-interest code
(and pursuantto section 87302) until they are covered under new conflict-of-interest
codes. Such an approach ensures there is a reduced gap in reporting by existing
employees. This conclusion furthers the purposes of the Act and miets with the goals of
sections 87300-87312. (Matuoka Advice Letter, I-05-148, copy enclosed.) Therefore,
based on the provisions cited in water code sectionTga2z@l and (g) and the


File No. A-06-176
Page 6
Commission's pievious advice, CDBA staff members and consultants wtro transfer to
other agencies but perform essentially the same duties may continue to file annual
statements.
Next, we tum to the issue of whether a single conflict-of-interest code may be
adopted for the CALFED Program. You note that your preference, for purposes of
administrative convenience, would be that a single code be adopted by the ^R.ro*.",
Agency, covering CAlFED-dedicated positions and consultants, irrespective of the
CALFED agency to which the position is allocated or the agency which has entered into
the consultancy contract. However, section 8730l requires conflict-of-interest codes to
be formulated at the most decentralizedlevelpossible, but without precluding intra-
departmental review. Any question of the level of a department which should be deemed
arr"agency" for purposes of section 87300 must be resolved by the code reviewing body.
Therefore, we conclude that the agencies which received the positions transferred from
CDBA should amend their conflict-of-interest codes to include these positions if they are
making or participating in the making of governmental positions. Another factor we have
considered in reaching this conclusion is that it would be unduly burdensome on filing
officers for the other agencies to perform their filing officer duties for two separate
confl ict-of-interest codes.
The final question you raise is concerning the SEI filing duties of the CBDA
board members. The board members should continue to file annual statements under
CBDA's conflict-of-interest code. Since the agencies that received the transferred
positions will be amending their conflict-of-interest codes to include the newly-
transferred positions, CBDA must amend its conflict-of-interest code to remove positions
that have transferred to other agencies.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
't )*ry*x-
By: Dixie Howard
Manager, Filing Officer programs
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure

CBDA's conflict-of-interest code. CBDA must amend its conflict-of-interest code to
remove positions that have transferred to other agencies.

conflict of interest codes to include the newly-transferred positions.
2 and 3. Non-board member public officials and consultants transferring from
CBDA to other agencies but with no material change in job duties, should continue to file

4. CBDA board members should continue to file annual statements under
CBDA's conflict-of-interest code. CBDA must amend its conflict-of-interest code to
remove positions that have transferred to other agencies.
